concerns.text: Task T025 specifies implementing a 'Paired t-test' on CV scores. The Spec (FR-005) and Constitution (Principle VII) explicitly mandate a 'corrected resampled t-test (Nadeau & Bengio)'. A standard paired t-test on CV scores is statistically invalid due to data reuse and violates the statistical rigor constraint. This task description relaxes the required statistical method.

Task T027 describes mapping bits 'within a defined radius' of the phosphorus atom. The Spec (SC-003) and Constitution (Principle VI) strictly define the metric as bits 'within radius 2'. Using a variable or undefined radius violates the specific success criterion and the structural integrity constraint.

Task T018 describes the split logic but omits the explicit requirement to 'halt execution' if the 20-sample threshold with Tanimoto < 0.85 cannot be met. The Spec (Edge Cases) and Constitution (Principle VII) mandate a hard halt and invalid report generation, not just a log message. The task description risks implementing a soft failure or threshold relaxation.

Task T030 instructs to 'skip t-test' if n < 50. The Spec (Edge Cases) requires the system to 'flag a Low Sample Size warning... and skip the statistical significance test... recording this limitation in the final report'. The task description lacks the explicit instruction to record the limitation in the final report, risking a silent skip without documentation.
